title: Ruehssia haitiensis

The Annonaceae family is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as custard apples or soursops. The classification of Ruehssia haitiensis is further supported by its characteristics and morphology, which are typical of the Annonaceae family. intro: Ruehssia haitiensis, also known as Haitian rue, is a plant species belonging to the Annonaceae family, native to Haiti and other Caribbean islands. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, making it useful for treating various health conditions such as wounds, fever, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Ruehssia haitiensis include annonaceous acetogenins, which have been shown to exhibit potent cytotoxic and anti-cancer properties. Ruehssia haitiensis is commonly prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, and is traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat a range of ailments, including digestive issues and skin problems.
